summary:
Claim: Jersey finger is caused by a rupture of the flexor digitorum profundus tendon.  

Evidence: - The flexor digitorum profundus tendon inserts into the distal phalanx, placing it under high tensile stress during gripping activities. - Sudden forces, such as catching an unexpected object, can cause this tendon to tear due to its anatomical position and mechanical vulnerability.  

Answer: B
